servinfo.title: Precautions for Handling of Front Strut

attention1.ptxt: Since a high-pressure gas is sealed inside each front strut (1), there is a risk of personal injury if you do not take proper precautions when handling them.

item.ptxt: Do not disassemble any front strut.
Do not put a front strut into any fire.
Do not store a front strut where it gets hot.

Before disposing of a front strut, be sure to drill an approximately 3 mm (0.12 in.) diameter hole (2) in it at the point indicated by the arrow in the figure and let the gas and oil out of the cylinder.
Be sure to wear eye protection when drilling a gas release hole. Although the gas itself is harmless, metal chips produced by the drilling can injure your eyes.
